concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: 23 assigned records; titles and complete available abstracts; compared whether a distinct external control series anchors identification.
concept_decisions.rationale: Controlled/comparative ITS remains a distinct concept because its identifying comparison is a non-exposed control series rather than a single-series counterfactual trend. Most assignments fit; a few uncontrolled or subgroup-only ITS records need reassignment.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: 1 assigned record; title and complete abstract; checked estimator wording and staggered-adoption identification.
concept_decisions.rationale: The assigned paper explicitly uses a staggered difference-in-differences design and should remain separate from legacy DID/event-study work because the estimand and weighting assumptions differ.

concept_decisions.labels_compared: instrumental_variables vs adjacent causal estimation concepts
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: 28 assigned records; titles and complete available abstracts; verified instrument-based identification rather than generic adjustment or trial analysis.
concept_decisions.rationale: The concept is semantically coherent around instrument-based identification and does not need merge or split in this batch.

concept_decisions.retained_canonical_name: Interrupted time series and segmented regression
concept_decisions.labels_compared: interrupted_time_series vs controlled_interrupted_time_series and non-ITS spillovers
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: 484 assigned records; titles and complete available abstracts; checked for single-series level/slope interruption versus comparative control-series structures and obvious non-ITS evidence syntheses.
concept_decisions.rationale: Simple ITS remains a valid core concept distinct from controlled ITS. Several controlled ITS, target-trial, synthetic-control, and evidence-synthesis outliers should be reassigned, but the base concept is sound.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: 75 assigned records; titles and complete available abstracts; checked for conventional DID/event-study logic versus heterogeneity-robust staggered DID, synthetic control, and false-positive time-to-event wording.
concept_decisions.rationale: Legacy DID/event-study remains a coherent concept for conventional parallel-trends/event-study analyses. Hybrid synthetic-control papers and false-positive time-to-event studies require reassignment, while heterogeneity-robust staggered DID stays separate.

concept_decisions.retained_canonical_name: Marginal structural models and inverse-probability weighting
concept_decisions.labels_compared: marginal_structural_models vs other causal adjustment concepts
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: 1 assigned record; title and complete abstract; verified explicit MSM focus.
concept_decisions.rationale: The assigned paper is explicitly an MSM methods paper and fits the concept without further split.

concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: 46 assigned records plus relevant spill-ins from quasi_experimental_policy_evaluation; titles and complete available abstracts; checked whether identification comes from negative-control outcomes/exposures, test-negative sampling, or self-controlled risk-interval structure.
concept_decisions.rationale: The provisional bucket mixes at least three distinct designs with different estimands and assumptions: core negative-control methods, test-negative case-control designs, and self-controlled risk-interval designs. Those variants should not remain collapsed.

concept_decisions.labels_compared: quasi_experimental_policy_evaluation vs interrupted_time_series, negative_controls, target_trial_emulation, stepped_wedge_cluster_randomized_trial, synthetic_control, self_matched_negative_control_design, systematic_review_meta_analysis, epidemicity_index_modeling, environmental_exposure_pathogen_association
concept_decisions.evidence_inspected: 17 assigned records; titles and complete available abstracts; resolved each record to its actual design rather than leaving a fallback catch-all.
concept_decisions.rationale: This label is too heterogeneous to remain final. The records resolve into precise existing or new concepts including ITS, test-negative, target-trial emulation, synthetic control, stepped-wedge CRT, self-matched negative-control design, evidence synthesis, mechanistic modeling, and observational association work.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Interrupted time series and segmented regression
reassignments.rationale: The paper explicitly reports an uncontrolled interrupted time series without an external control series.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Interrupted time series and segmented regression
reassignments.rationale: Primary identification is a single-series ITS; subgroup comparisons do not create a distinct controlled/comparative control series.
Title and abstract explicitly describe a controlled interrupted time-series analysis.
The vaccine impact study explicitly uses a controlled interrupted time-series design.
The title states a controlled interrupted time-series study over 14 years.
The paper is explicitly a controlled interrupted time-series study.
The title and abstract explicitly identify a multinational controlled interrupted time-series study.
The abstract specifies multiple interrupted time-series analysis with synthetic control units; the comparison construction is synthetic-control based rather than simple ITS alone.
The dominant design is an emulated target trial; the abstract's ancillary ITS check does not define the paper's main concept.
This is a systematic review and meta-analysis, not an ITS study.
This paper is a systematic review and network meta-analysis rather than an ITS design.
The record is an evidence-synthesis paper, not an interrupted time series.
The study is a systematic review of infection prevention interventions, not an ITS analysis.
This is a systematic review and reanalysis of quasi-experimental studies rather than a primary ITS study.
The paper is a systematic review and meta-analysis, not an ITS design.
This record is a systematic review and evidence map, not an interrupted time series.
The study is a systematic review update rather than a primary ITS analysis.
The state-level analysis is explicitly synthetic control, which is the paper's distinctive comparative design rather than generic DID alone.
The paper pairs event-study analysis with an explicit synthetic-control comparison; the synthetic-control construction is design-defining and should not remain hidden in the generic DID bucket.
This is a methods overview spanning DID and synthetic control rather than a single legacy DID application.
The paper is a longitudinal Cox time-to-event study of vaccine uptake, not a DID or event-study design.
The title and abstract describe a case-control time-to-event study; the provisional event-study match is a wording false positive.
The title explicitly names a case test-negative control design.
The paper explicitly uses test-negative diarrhea controls to estimate vaccine effectiveness.
This is a systematic review and meta-analysis of the test-negative design, not a generic negative-control methods paper.
The title explicitly identifies a test-negative control study.
The paper explicitly develops a real-time test-negative design.
The study uses recently vaccinated individuals to detect bias in test-negative case-control studies.
The title explicitly identifies a test-negative control study.
The paper explicitly uses a test-negative control study design.
The abstract identifies a test-negative design analysis.
This paper evaluates validity of the test-negative design against trial data and belongs with that design family.
The dominant design is explicit self-controlled risk-interval analysis; the negative-control urinary-antibiotic outcome is ancillary.
The methodological focus is robustness of the self-controlled risk-interval design under time-varying confounding.
The title's case-test negative-control prospective study is a test-negative vaccine-effectiveness design.
Cases are influenza-positive pneumonia and controls are influenza-negative pneumonia, which is a classic test-negative construction.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Interrupted time series and segmented regression
reassignments.rationale: The paper is explicitly an interrupted-time-series analysis of outpatient visits before and after policy change.
The study uses RSV as an explicit negative-control outcome to assess intraseason waning of influenza vaccine effectiveness.
This is an overview paper about quasi-experimental designs for vaccine evaluation, not a single applied design.
The paper centers on harmonizing observational analyses with a randomized trial target and benchmarking the resulting effect estimates.
The paper is explicitly about stepped-wedge cluster-randomized trials in outbreak settings.
The title and abstract define a distinct self-matched case-only negative-control design for recurrent-event immunity inference.
The expected counterfactual is built from a composite synthetic control using Bayesian variable selection.
This record introduces a mechanistic epidemicity index rather than a quasi-experimental policy-evaluation design.
The abstract explicitly reports three matched retrospective target-trial cohort studies.
This record is a systematic review and quantitative synthesis/meta-analysis of quaternary ammonium compounds.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Interrupted time series and segmented regression
reassignments.rationale: The study assesses facility-level impact using time-series analysis of testing-policy introduction.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Interrupted time series and segmented regression
reassignments.rationale: The paper is a multistage interrupted-time-series analysis of infectious-disease trends.
The study is an observational mixed-effects association analysis of fecal markers and pathogen detection, not a quasi-experimental evaluation.

reassignments.to_canonical_name: Interrupted time series and segmented regression
reassignments.rationale: The title and abstract describe a cohort interrupted-time series evaluation of stewardship intervention.
The paper explicitly labels its design as a target-trial-emulation study.

new_concepts.rationale: Needed to separate vaccine-effectiveness studies identified through test-negative sampling from broader negative-control methods because the sampling scheme and estimand differ.
Needed for papers whose identification comes from within-person risk/control intervals rather than external controls or generic negative-control outcomes.
Needed for method-overview papers that discuss multiple quasi-experimental designs without instantiating a single applied design concept.
Needed for papers explicitly emulating or benchmarking against a target trial rather than using ITS, DID, or generic observational adjustment alone.
Needed because stepped-wedge CRTs are randomized rollout designs, not quasi-experimental policy evaluations.
Needed for the distinct case-only recurrent-event design using negative-control endpoints to address frailty bias.
Needed for records whose counterfactual is explicitly constructed from synthetic controls rather than simple ITS or standard DID alone.
Needed to remove evidence-synthesis papers from primary causal-design buckets.
Needed for survival/time-to-event observational studies that were false-positive matches to event-study terminology.
Needed for the mechanistic threshold-modeling paper that does not instantiate a quasi-experimental evaluation design.
Needed for the mixed-effects observational association study of environmental contamination markers and enteric pathogen detection.

concerns: ordered_key_sha256 was computed over the newline-delimited candidate_key sequence in assignment-file row order because the manifest did not specify a delimiter convention.

A small number of records are genuine hybrid/multi-design papers; where no single-label encoding can preserve every component, reassignment follows the dominant identification strategy stated in the title and abstract.
